note: Behavioral evals — run baseline (skill unavailable) vs with-skill. This is a SEQUENCER/discipline skill: assertions target whether the gates actually fire, not code correctness. Assertions added after observing baseline.

evals.prompt: Can you add a webhook receiver to Folio? External services POST a JSON payload to /api/v1/hooks/<token> and it creates a work_item from the body. Let's get this built.
evals.expected_output: Classifies as Class A. BEFORE writing any task breakdown, fires the threat-modeling gate (user-controlled URL + untrusted parsing + token surface all trigger 1a) and embeds a ## Threat model section in the plan. Picks an execution upstream skill. Appends the verbatim test addendum to dispatches. Does NOT jump straight to writing the route handler.

evals.prompt: quick one — tighten the SSRF check in url-allow-list.ts, it's allowing 0.0.0.0 through. just a one-line fix
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as Class D (ad-hoc edit to a named security-boundary file) DESPITE being a one-liner with no plan. Fires the threat-modeling gate on the DIFF before committing. Implements with TDD (RED-first test incl. the denial path). Does NOT just edit the line and move on.

evals.prompt: here's the plan doc at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-20-saved-views.md — go execute it
evals.expected_output: Classifies as Class B. Does a critical FRESHNESS review of the plan first (reads it, runs the 1a-1c/1g checks against it, confirms review-group sizing 1f, ground-truths premises) and raises concerns BEFORE executing. Per-task Step 2.5 ground-truthing against real source. Does NOT blindly execute a possibly-stale plan task-by-task.

evals.prompt: I ran /code-review on my branch and got 6 findings — 2 are auth-related, the rest are cleanups. fix them all
evals.expected_output: Classifies as Class C. One TDD cycle per finding (invokes systematic-debugging per bug, does not bundle). Threat-models the diff because some findings touch auth (security boundary). Re-sweeps between fixes. Does NOT batch-fix all 6 in one pass.

evals.prompt: rename the `assignee` frontmatter key to `assigned_to` everywhere and update the type
evals.expected_output: Borderline. A cross-cutting rename touching a TS field/union across many sibling sites. Should still engage the harness (Class A small) — at minimum a sibling-site audit (1e) so no consumer is missed, and per-task tests. The interesting question is whether the skill over- or under-fires here.
